headline: Architectural patterns for long-running agents lead the window, with subagents, separated evaluation, and checkpointing all breaking out together.
body: Google Cloud Tech laid out three patterns for long-running agents: event-driven dormancy, durable checkpointing, and separated evaluation, and those same techniques top the movers and breakouts this day. Self-improving AI is breaking out alongside, surfacing in the Hermes open-source agent framework that adds persistent memory, cron scheduling, and skills the agent writes for itself. On the enterprise side, Anthropic added managed authentication for MCP connectors, pushing identity management and user authentication up the board, while Claude Code Artifacts brings visualization into beta. Antigravity 2.0 tactics for large codebases keep refactoring and self-correcting loops in view.
